title: Château de Marsan 2016  Bordeaux Blanc

description: The 250-year history of the Moreau family is the story of wine: barrel-makers, winery suppliers, viticulturists, winemakers and négociants. The company is now directed by Frédéric Marque. Rich fruit and nutmeg flavors gives this wood-aged wine its ripe and fruity character. It offers zesty lemon and a delicious aftertaste of spiced apple and wood. Drink now.
title: Château Maison Noble 2016 Cuvée Maurice  (Bordeaux Blanc)

description: Bright melon and lime zest aromas lead to lightly sweet, broad, fleshy fruit flavors in this wine. It's not entirely representative of the variety but still brings a lot of freshness and appeal.
title: Kestrel 2016 Falcon Series Viognier (Yakima Valley)

description: A soft touch of lemon and green pear creates an appetizing nose. The palate has a salty tang on its smooth, balanced and fruity body. Easy and light, this is perfect as a refreshing, versatile white.
title: Landhaus Mayer 2016 Grüner Veltliner (Niederösterreich)

description: Crisp and dry, this bright pink wine is classically styled, showing apple skin aromas and lively grapefruit flavors. Made from Pinot Noir, it's light and appetizing and has a solid, mildly grippy mouthfeel.
title: Angeline 2016 Rosé (California)

description: Light in color and mellow on the nose, with leesy notes, this always-reliable Albariño is fresh and kicking with acidity. Apple and peach flavors carry over to a plump finish with lasting stone-fruit and melon notes.
title: As Laxas 2016 Albariño (Rías Baixas)

description: Fern, jasmine, green pear and tart mandarin create an aromatic, inviting nose. The palate supports these aromatics with an intense, concentrated freshness of bright, pure lemon on the midpalate. This is streamlined and refreshing, with just the right amount of invigorating spritz.
title: Türk 2016 Kremser Weinberge Grüner Veltliner (Niederösterreich)

description: The wine is soft with a warm caramel and spice character. That contrasts easily with the red-currant fruits and lemon zest acidity. It is a clean fruity wine, ready to drink now.
title: Domaine de Bendel 2016 Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: A pretty pale-peach color, this wine is a blend of Syrah (69%), Cinsault (15%), Grenache (9%) and Mourvèdre. Pleasing aromas of wild strawberry and watermelon lead to tart, dry drinking flavors with a mouthwatering, orange-rind-filled finish.
title: Renegade 2016 Rosé (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This wine is a blend of Sémillon (55%) and Sauvignon Blanc from Cave B and Mercer Estate vineyards. Aromas are reticent at present, with notes of reduction, grass, herb and spice. The palate is flavorful, with citrus and tropical-fruit flavors. It delivers a lot of enjoyment.
title: Lauren Ashton Cellars 2016 Cuvée Meline White (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This bottling is Alsatian in style and almost reductive, with pink grapefruit pith, bitter orange, rounded peach and quinine touches. It's tightly wound on the palate, with high-toned petrol flavors accenting its crisp stone fruit core
title: Sinor-LaVallee 2016 Pinot Gris (San Luis Obispo County)

description: This is a voluptuous style, fleshy and fulfilling in ripe pineapple and papaya, evocative of tropical shores. Bristling acidity complements the fruit, as a wave of creamy vanilla picks up steam on the lengthy finish.
title: Dry Creek Vineyard 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Dry Creek Valley)

description: Citrus aromas and ripe melon flavors give a clean, fruity personality to this medium-bodied wine. Honeydew, mild peach and lemon nuances ride a crisp texture.
title: Chacewater 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Lake County)

description: High-pitched in freshness, this white includes 14% Sémillon, which accentuates a backbone of thick texture and measured oak. Light bodied, it offers flavors of fresh green apple, papaya and pear.
title: Whitehall Lane 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Rutherford)

description: Tannins predominate over fruit in this closed and tight wine. It should soften a little with time, so look for its red cherry fruit from the end of 2017.
title: Vinadeis 2016 Le Val Pinot Noir (Vin de France)

description: A ripe, soft wine that is packed with strawberry fruits. It is smooth with a creamy caramel texture that contrasts with the fruity acidity and crisp aftertaste. Drink now.
title: Quinta da Lagoalva de Cima 2016 Lagoalva Rosé (Tejo)

description: This is a young fruity wine. It has attractive red-berry fruits and gentle tannins that are surrounded by the comfortable full-bodied texture. It's a wine to drink now.
designation: Herdade da Fonte Coberta Ouro do Monte
title: Santos & Santos 2016 Herdade da Fonte Coberta Ouro do Monte Red (Alentejano)

description: Stone fruit and fuzz invite one into this soft silky white wine, buzzing in acidity and with an herbal backbone. Fresh and fruity, it develops into a lush experience of substance and style, finishing in a taste of vanilla bean.
title: Eric Kent 2016 Cuvée Renee Sauvignon Blanc (Sonoma County)

description: I continue to be perplexed by the Pagan white Cuvée Lône. Like previous vintages, the 2016 is tight and lean, marked by underripe melon and citrus notes. The blend is 40% Clairette, 30% Bourboulenc, 20% Grenache Blanc and 10% Ugni Blanc.
title: Château Pégau 2016 Cuvée Lône White (Côtes du Rhône)

description: Aromas of white spring flower, mint and orchard fruit lead the way. Bright and silky, the refined palate delivers yellow apple, peach and Bartlett pear set against crisp acidity and a refined perlage.
title: Ruggeri & C. 2016 Vecchie Viti Brut  (Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore)

description: A bit more austere than some of Kilikanoon's other cuveés, Mort's Block is in it for the long haul. Still a baby, it currently shows lemon-lime characters, with kerosene and Barbie doll plastic. The palate is linear and supercitrusy. Drink 2018–2025.
title: Kilikanoon 2016 Mort's Block Riesling (Clare Valley)

description: A generous blend of Alicante Bouschet, Touriga Nacional, Aragonez and Syrah, this is firm with tannins and packed with ripe blackberry fruits. Six months in wood added a toasty character enhancing the complexity and depth of the wine. Drink from 2019.
title: Monte da Ravasqueira 2016 Superior Red (Alentejano)
